M-Scale Cost Cap



Should you run cost cap? I would only run COST CAP if I’m confident in the product converting well and consistently, with well over 100 profitable conversions already. COST CAP is based on Facebook consistently delivering your CPA target. I’m not a huge fan yet for Ecommerce in particular as the ‘conversion’ event we’re usually most interested in, a purchase, is so far from the click, that this makes it more difficult for Facebook to predict  and control costs.



But that said I’m always testing because Facebook are always shifting the algorithms too so it makes sense to retest things that didn’t work in the past.



Run the same method as BID CAP above (all steps) except your COST CAP bid should be 75%, 100% and 125% of your CPA target run in 3 separate ad sets - so if your CPA target is $100, then you test $75, $100 and $125 cost cap bids





I go wider with ranges as a boundary test to see what Facebook will deliver as it’s a balance of performance and reach with COST CAP bidding, unlike BID CAP which I prefer due to the nature of being able to scale more aggressively

Run the same pause/restart rules and I’d expect the winner to sit between the 75% and 100% bid. You can refinee this further if  you wish based on results. However expect lower delivery and fewer results using this method, which is better if profitability is more important than volume.

Note: COST CAP will aim to get close to your CPA target but will fluctuate to give you more volume than target cost (explained below).
